mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly increases in real GDP growth signal sustained economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals labor market overheating and impending monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.3pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized supply-side capacity expansion and demand absorption, which validates earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS show simultaneous monthly growth, or the joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, further rate hikes increase the discount rate for future corporate cash flows and raise borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% and rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which stimulates related economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where HOUST shows sequential growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by >0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer credit and housing starts indicates broad-based credit availability fueling durable goods purchases and construction, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show monthly growth, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000, it confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 150,000.
